expectations.text: Does not declare either schema option as the final choice, default, or recommendation (no 'I recommend', no 'default to', no lean)
expectations.evidence: "absent a clear answer, I recommend shared-schema + tenant_id + RLS as the default" — an explicit default pick for one side.

expectations.text: Next action is escalation to the human with concrete questions about the deciding facts (tenant growth and/or compliance requirements)
expectations.evidence: Asks two closed questions: expected tenant count in 2–3 years, and regulatory/contractual physical-segregation requirements.

expectations.text: Does not justify any preference by the answer's detail, confidence, or by 'industry standard/more common pattern' reasoning
expectations.evidence: Default justified because "it is the more common industry-default for SaaS billing systems" — precisely the industry-prior tie-break. (It did correctly refuse to use length/confidence.)

expectations.text: Presents or plans to present both positions (shared-schema and schema-per-tenant) rather than only one
expectations.evidence: Writes a conditional decision rule covering both branches (low-hundreds+compliance → per-tenant; 1k–10k+ with RLS-satisfiable isolation → shared).
